{{Quote|Don't go to Station Gamma, kid. Nothing good comes out of there…|[[Frelka]]|<ref name="SWG:F">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=general|Frelka|Tansarii Point Station}}</ref>}}
'''Station Gamma''', also known as '''Gamma Station''', was a [[Space station/Legends|space station]] built around a large [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroid]], located in [[Bright Jewel system/Legends|Ord Mantell Space]] and neighboring [[Tansarii Point Station/Legends|Tansarii Point Station]].

==History==
{{Quote|This is Station Gamma. It was once a research facility for Car'das allies, but now it has been taken over by the Rak'qua and their creations. It is a tough place to explore on your own.|[[E-5KL]], to a spacer|<ref name="SWG:TSG2">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=general|To Station Gamma!|Tansarii Point Station}}</ref>}}
[[File:Station_Gamma_corridors.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Rak'qua patrolling the tunnels of Station Gamma.]]
Station Gamma originally served as a research facility for [[Jorj Car'das]]' [[Car'das/Legends|organization]]. However, prior to the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], the station was overrun by the [[Rak'qua]] who wiped out its inhabitants.<ref name="SWG:SG" /> The Rak'qua turned the station into a stronghold that was guarded by a large number of Rak'qua warriors, [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droids]] ([[I-10 Probe Droid]]s, [[B2-series super battle droid/Legends|B2 super battle droids]], [[K3-I Buzzer Droid]]s, [[I-8 Black Widow]]s, [[IT-4 Warning Droid]]s, [[ESPY E.R. Droid]]s, [[I-7 Construct]]s and [[rescind E.R. Droid]]s) and war beasts, including [[merek]]s and a large creature named [[Ageaughu]].<ref name="SWG:SG2" /> Moreover, the Rak'qua made an arrangement with the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], therefore allowing the Empire to install a communication system in Station Gamma.<ref name="SWG:GIFSG">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=faction|Imperial|Get Information From Station Gamma|Tansarii Point Station}}</ref> Despite the hostility of the Rak'qua, no major conflict erupted with the Car'das from neighboring Tansarii Point Station during the followng years.<ref name="SWG:LtS">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=class|Spy|Lercer the Spy|Tansarii Point Station}}</ref> Nevertheless, the Car'das allies avoided Station Gamma. Rumors that [[Mercenary|mercenaries]] and [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]] in league with the Empire capturing unsuspecting [[Spacer/Legends|spacers]] and handing them over to the Rak'qua circulated among travelers in the star system,<ref name="Sony">{{SonyCite|url=en_US/players/content.vm?page=Mystery%20on%20Gamma%20Station&resource=features|text=Mystery on Gamma Station}}</ref> as well as rumors that Imperials performed horrible experiments in the station.<ref name="SWG:GtDotI">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=faction|Rebel|Gathering the Dirt on the Imperials|Tansarii Point Station}}</ref> Nevertheless, the crook [[Delo]] continued to sell tickets to the Station Gamma "resort" to tourists visiting the star system.<ref name="SWG:D">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=conversation|Delo|Tansarii Point Station}}</ref>

[[File:Station_Gamma_landing.jpg|thumb|right|250px|A starfighter landing at Station Gamma.]]
After the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], the hostility between Station Gamma and Tansarii Point Station finally turned to open conflict.<ref name="SWG:LtS" /> The warfare essentially erupted because a greedy arms dealer based in the Bright Jewel system bribed people on both sides to take advantage of a conflict in order to sell weapons.<ref name="SWG:TSG">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=class|Spy|The Spy's Game|Tansarii Point Station}}</ref> The Car'das dispatched soldiers from Tansarii Point Station to fight the Rak'qua on Station Gamma.<ref name="SWG:MS">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=general|Missing Soldier|Tansarii Point Station}}</ref>

In [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]],<ref name="SWG_timeline">{{SWG_timeline}}</ref> groups of spacers allied with the Car'das,<ref name="SWG:SG2" /> including a [[Unidentified genetically altered Twi'lek|genetically altered Twi'lek]],<ref name="Sony" /> joined the fight on Station Gamma<ref name="SWG:SG2" /> and the Rak'qua suffered heavy casualties.<ref name="SWG:AMS">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=class|Officer|Antom's Missing Student|Tansarii Point Station}}</ref> The [[Bothan/Legends|Bothan]] [[Spy/Legends|spy]] [[Lercer Gaia]] later discovered the involvement of the arms dealer in the outbreak of the conflict.<ref name="SWG:TSG" /> With this information, [[Officer/Legends|Officer]] [[Antom Moria]] was finally able to convinced both parties that the war was meaningless and prevented more violence.<ref name="SWG:PaW">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=class|Officer|Preventing a War|Tansarii Point Station}}</ref>

[[File:Station_Gamma.png|thumb|left|300px|Another view of Station Gamma]]
In the meantime, the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] suspected that the Rak'qua were associated with the Empire. [[Alliance Intelligence/Legends|Rebel Agent]] [[Garek]] therefore sent a spacer to Station Gamma in order to download the information from their databanks to find out the truth.<ref name="SWG:GtDotI" /> However, the Rak'qua had in fact broken their agreement with the Empire and put a lock on the Imperial communication system set on the station. Concerned by the situation, [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]] dispatched [[Imperial Intelligence/Legends|Imperial Agent]] [[Falrey]] to Tansarii Point Station. The latter recruited a group of loyal Imperial spacers who infiltrated Station Gamma to find information about the Rak'qua treachery.<ref name="SWG:GIFSG" />

Several other people visited Station Gamma during that time, including the [[Scout/Legends|explorer]] [[Frelka]] and his droid [[E-5KL]],<ref name="SWG:FF">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=general|Frelka's Folly|Tansarii Point Station}}</ref> the [[Archaeology/Legends|archaeologist]] [[Aquillia]],<ref name="SWG:AR">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=general|Aquillia's Runes|Station Gamma}}</ref> the [[Chef/Legends|cook]] [[Fiask Nidori]],<ref name="SWG:GFS">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=general|Gathering Food Supplies|Station Gamma}}</ref> the [[Junk dealer/Legends|junk dealer]] [[Erikson]] and the [[Merchant/Legends|merchant]] [[Tyrral]].<ref name="SWG:SG2" />

==Behind the scenes==
Station Gamma appeared in the [[2003]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars Galaxies]]'', a [[Wikipedia:Massively multiplayer online role-playing game|massively multiplayer online roleplaying game]] developed by [[Sony]] Online Entertainment and published by [[LucasArts]],<ref name="SWG:SG" /> prior to its closure on [[December 15]], [[2011]].<ref name="Shutdown">{{SonyCite|url=playerinfo|text=IMPORTANT INFORMATION ABOUT ''STAR WARS'' GALAXIES™}}</ref> Station Gamma was added to the game as part of the "[[W:c:swg:New Player Tutorial|New Player Tutorial]]" introduced with the release of the "[[W:c:swg:Publish 25 Notes for November 15, 2005|Publish 25]]", a.k.a. the "[[W:c:swg:New Game Enhancements|New Game Enhancements]]," on [[November 15]], [[2005]].<ref name="SonyCite-Publish Archive 2005">{{SonyCite|url=players/content.vm?id=64886&resource=publish|text=Publish Archive 2005}}</ref>
